top_signals.title: U.S. Turns Cold War Plutonium Into Nuclear Fuel
top_signals.summary: The federal U.S. government has turned to plutonium from the Cold War era as an alternative to uranium in nuclear power generation, as supply of the default nuclear fuel runs short of demand. Washington has already selected five companies to supply the fuel to, as nuclear returns to the energy spotlight. The Energy Department said this month it would discuss the use of plutonium as nuclear fuel with five nuclear energy companies seeking to build new generation capacity in the country. The reason that the DoE is considering plutonium is the tight…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: Oil Edges Lower on Tentative Deal to Extend US-Iranian Truce
top_signals.summary: Oil edged lower after the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days, according to a person familiar with the matter, potentially allowing shipments through the Strait of Hormuz to resume.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Japan and Philippines agree to elevate ties to new level amid China concerns
top_signals.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi also said they would begin formal negotiations on a key intelligence-sharing pact.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Trump’s Least Bad Option in Iran
top_signals.summary: The logic behind a limited deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Coming Crisis of NATO Deterrence
top_signals.summary: Nuclear guarantees cannot replace U.S. forces in Europe.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
